---
title: ""
author: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-expand: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    self-contained: true
    code-fold: true
    output-file: "ESM_1"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
execute: 
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

# Project Setup

```{r}
#| label:  setup
library(emmeans)
library(corrplot)
library(multcomp)
library(FSA)
library(agricolae)
library(tidyverse)
library(car)
library(lme4)
library(googlesheets4)
library(factoextra)
library(FactoMineR)
library(GerminaR)
source("https://inkaverse.com/setup.r")
```

# Data import

```{r}
gs <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1GgfIOmTGyvjtuVQzJgFA8ueD1yqRh7x7gyueLdQVd90/edit?usp=sharing" %>%
  as_sheets_id()

germination <- gs %>%
  range_read(ss = ., sheet = "STable2") %>%
  mutate(across(1:9, ~ as.factor(.)))

glimpse(germination)

inoculation <- gs %>%
  range_read(ss = ., sheet = "STable3") %>%
  mutate(across(1:6, ~ as.factor(.)))

glimpse(inoculation)

climate <- gs %>%
  range_read(ss = ., sheet = "STable4") %>%
  mutate(across(1:2, ~ as.factor(.)))

glimpse(climate)
```

# Data summary

Summary of the number of data points recorded for each treatment and evaluated variable.

```{r}
sm <- germination %>%
  group_by(Inoculant) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("ger"), ~ sum(!is.na(.))))

sm %>% kable(align = "c")

sm <- inoculation %>%
  group_by(Site, treat) %>%
  summarise(across(pl_height:yield, ~ sum(!is.na(.))))

sm %>% kable(align = "c")
```

# Research Objective

1. To evaluate the effects of bio-inoculation and PEG 6000 stress on pea seed biomass, weight, and germination on pea crop.

> Figura: Proceso germinativo de arverja inoculados co bioestimulantes en condicioens de estres hídrico simulado. (a) Gerinación (%). (b) Tiempo medio de Germinación (c) Sincronia de la Germinación. (d) Indice de vigor de la germinación
(e) Peso se las radiculas

> PCA germinación

1. To demonstrate the combined effects of bio-inoculation and fertilization on field experiment on pea crop

- Correlación de las variables por ambiente
- Una MLT y calcular las medias ajustadas (BLUEs)

> Figura: Las variables de campo

> Figura: PCA

## RO 1

To evaluate the effects of bio-inoculation and PEG 6000 stress on pea seed biomass, weight, and germination on pea crop.

### Germination (%)

#### Standard germination

```{r}
fb <- germination %>%
  select(sort, Inoculant,peg, block, seeds, starts_with("ger"))

gerdr <- fb

prefix <- "ger_"
dtnames <- gerdr %>% names()
evalprf <- dtnames[grepl(prefix, dtnames)]

gercount <- gerdr %>% 
  select(starts_with(prefix)) %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  summarize(.data[[evalprf[1]]], d = list(diff(c_across(everything())))) %>% 
  unnest_wider(d, names_sep="", names_repair=\(x) evalprf) %>% 
  cbind(gerdr %>% select(!starts_with(prefix)), .) %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(. < 0, 0, .)))
  
# gercount %>% web_table()
```

#### Performe ten germination indices

```{r}
gercount %>%
  head(10) %>%
  kable(caption = "Germination dataset", align = "c")

glimpse(gercount)

# germination analysis (ten variables)

gsm <- gercount %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  ger_summary(SeedN = "seeds"
                   , evalName = "ger_"
                   , data = .
                   )

# Pea data set processed

gsm %>% 
  head(10) %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2))) %>% 
  kable(caption = "Function ger_summary performe ten germination indices")
```

#### Germination Percentage (GRP)

```{r}
# analysis of variance

av <- lmer(grp ~ 1 + (1|block) + Inoculant*peg, data = gsm)

Anova(av, type = 3, test.statistic = "F")

mc1 <- emmeans(av, ~ peg|Inoculant) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig1 = ".group")

mc2 <- emmeans(av, ~ Inoculant|peg) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ toupper(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig2 = ".group")

mc <- merge(mc2, mc1) %>% 
  unite(col = "group", c("sig1", "sig2"), sep = "") %>% 
  arrange(Inoculant, desc(peg))

mc %>% kable(caption = "Germination percentage mean comparision")

grp <- mc %>% 
   fplot(data = .
       , type = "bar"
       , x = "Inoculant"
       , y = "emmean"
       , group = "peg"
       , ylimits = c(0, 200, 50)
       , ylab = "Germination ('%')"
       , xlab = "Inoculant"
       , glab = "PEG (MPa)"
       , error = "SE"
       , sig = "group"
       , color = T
       )

grp
```

#### Mean Germination Time (MGT)

```{r}
# analysis of variance

av <- lmer(mgt ~ 1 + (1|block) + Inoculant*peg, data = gsm)

Anova(av, type = 3, test.statistic = "F")

mc1 <- emmeans(av, ~ peg|Inoculant) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig1 = ".group")

mc2 <- emmeans(av, ~ Inoculant|peg) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ toupper(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig2 = ".group")

mc <- merge(mc2, mc1) %>% 
  unite(col = "group", c("sig1", "sig2"), sep = "")

mc %>% kable(caption = "Germination percentage mean comparision")

mgt <- mc %>% 
   fplot(data = .
       , type = "bar"
       , x = "Inoculant"
       , y = "emmean"
       , group = "peg"
       , ylimits = c(0, 6, 1)
       , ylab = "Mean germination time (days)"
       , xlab = "Inoculant"
       , glab = "PEG (MPa)"
       , error = "SE"
       , sig = "group"
       , color = T
       )

mgt
```

#### Synchronization Index (SYN)

```{r}
# analysis of variance

av <- lmer(syn ~ 1 + (1|block) + Inoculant*peg, data = gsm)

Anova(av, type = 3, test.statistic = "F")

mc1 <- emmeans(av, ~ peg|Inoculant) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig1 = ".group")

mc2 <- emmeans(av, ~ Inoculant|peg) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ toupper(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig2 = ".group")

mc <- merge(mc2, mc1) %>% 
  unite(col = "group", c("sig1", "sig2"), sep = "")

mc %>% kable(caption = "Germination percentage mean comparision")

syn <- mc %>% 
   fplot(data = .
       , type = "bar"
       , x = "Inoculant"
       , y = "emmean"
       , group = "peg"
       , ylimits = c(0, 2, 0.5)
       , ylab = "Synchronization Index"
       , xlab = "Inoculant"
       , glab = "PEG (MPa)"
       , error = "SE"
       , sig = "group"
       , color = T
       )

syn
```

#### Germination vigor index (GVI)

```{r}
# analysis of variance

weight_res <- germination %>%
  group_by(Inoculant, peg, block) %>%
  summarise(weight = mean(peso, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

gsm2 <- gsm %>%
  left_join(weight_res, by = c("Inoculant", "peg", "block")) %>%
  mutate(gv = weight * grp/100) %>% 
   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2)))

av <- lmer(gv ~ 1 + (1|block) + Inoculant*peg, data = gsm2)

Anova(av, type = 3, test.statistic = "F")

mc1 <- emmeans(av, ~ peg|Inoculant) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig1 = ".group")

mc2 <- emmeans(av, ~ Inoculant|peg) %>%
  cld(Letters = letters, reversed = T) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ trimws(.))) %>% 
  mutate(across(".group", ~ toupper(.))) %>% 
  rename(sig2 = ".group")

mc <- merge(mc2, mc1) %>% 
  unite(col = "group", c("sig1", "sig2"), sep = "")

mc %>% kable(caption = "Germination percentage mean comparision")

gvi <- mc %>% 
   fplot(data = .
       , type = "bar"
       , x = "Inoculant"
       , y = "emmean"
       , group = "peg"
       , ylimits = c(0, 10, 2)
       , ylab = "Germination vigor Index"
       , xlab = "Inoculant"
       , glab = "PEG (MPa)"
       , error = "SE"
       , sig = "group"
       , color = T
       )

gvi
```

#### Figure 2

Univariate analysis of the variables that determine the agronomic characteristics of mango.

```{r}
legend <- cowplot::get_plot_component(grp, 'guide-box-top', return_all = TRUE)

plot2 <- list(grp + theme(legend.position="none"
                                        , axis.title.x=element_blank()
                                        , axis.text.x=element_blank()
                                        , axis.ticks.x=element_blank())
           , mgt + theme(legend.position="none"
                                        , axis.title.x=element_blank()
                                        , axis.text.x=element_blank()
                                        , axis.ticks.x=element_blank())
           , syn + theme(legend.position="none")
           , gvi + theme(legend.position="none")
           ) %>% 
  plot_grid(plotlist = ., ncol = 2
            , labels = "auto"
            , rel_heights = c(1, 1)
            ) 

fig2 <- plot_grid(legend, plot2, ncol = 1, align = 'v', rel_heights = c(0.05, 1))

fig2 %>% 
  ggsave2(plot = ., "submission/Figure_2.jpg"
         , units = "cm"
         , width = 24
         , height = 16
         )

fig2 %>% 
  ggsave2(plot = ., "submission/Figure_2.eps"
         , units = "cm"
         , width = 24
         , height = 16
         )

knitr::include_graphics("submission/Figure_2.jpg")
```

#### Multivariate

Principal Component Analysis (PCA) of agronomic traits in the mango crop based on the use of rootstock-interstock combinations.

```{r}
mv <- gsm2 %>% 
  group_by(Inoculant, peg) %>%
  select(grp, mgt, syn, gv) %>% 
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), ~ mean(., na.rm = T))) %>%   
  unite("treat", Inoculant:peg, sep = " ") %>% 
  mutate(treat = paste0(treat, " MPa")) 
  
pca <- mv %>% 
  PCA(scale.unit = T, quali.sup = 1, graph = F) 

# summary

summary(pca, nbelements = Inf, nb.dec = 2)

f3a <- plot.PCA(x = pca, choix = "var"
                , cex=0.8
                , label = "var"
                )

f3b <- plot.PCA(x = pca, choix = "ind"
                , habillage = 1
                , invisible = c("ind")
                , cex=0.8
                , ylim = c(-3,3)
                , xlim = c(-4,4)
                ) 
```

#### Figure 3

Principal Component Analysis (PCA).

```{r}
fig <- list(f3a, f3b) %>% 
  plot_grid(plotlist = ., ncol = 2, nrow = 1
            , labels = "auto"
            , rel_widths = c(1, 1.3)
            ) 
fig %>% 
  ggsave2(plot = ., "submission/Figure_3.jpg", units = "cm"
          , width = 25, height = 10
          ) 

fig %>% 
  ggsave2(plot = ., "submission/Figure_3.eps", units = "cm"
          , width = 25, height = 10
          ) 

knitr::include_graphics("submission/Figure_3.jpg")
```

#### Correlation

```{r}
# ---- 1. Selecciona las variables de evaluación ----
vars_eval <- c("pl_height","days_flower", "pod_weight", "pod_n", "yield")

# ---- 2. Asegurar que las variables de interés sean numéricas antes de pivotar ----
df <- inoculation %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_eval), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(.)))))

# Separar datos por sitio
lamud <- df %>%
  filter(Site == "Lamud") %>%
  select(treat, block, all_of(vars_eval)) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(., "_Lamud"), all_of(vars_eval))

molino <- df %>%
  filter(Site == "Molinopampa") %>%
  select(treat, block, all_of(vars_eval)) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(., "_Molinopampa"), all_of(vars_eval))

# Unir por tratamiento y bloque
merged <- inner_join(lamud, molino, by = c("treat", "block"))

# Crear todas las combinaciones entre variables de Lamud y Molinopampa
corr_grid <- expand.grid(
  x = vars_eval,
  y = vars_eval,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calcular las correlaciones
corr_values <- map2_dfr(corr_grid$x, corr_grid$y, function(var_x, var_y) {
  col_x <- merged[[paste0(var_x, "_Lamud")]]
  col_y <- merged[[paste0(var_y, "_Molinopampa")]]
  
  if (is.numeric(col_x) && is.numeric(col_y)) {
    test <- cor.test(col_x, col_y, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
    tibble(
      x = var_x,
      y = var_y,
      r = test$estimate,
      p_value = test$p.value
    )
  } else {
    tibble(x = var_x, y = var_y, r = NA, p_value = NA)
  }
})

# Clasificar significancia
corr_values <- corr_values %>%
  mutate(
    signif = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "*", ""),
    label = sprintf("r = %.2f%s", r, signif)
  )

# --- Graficar mapa de calor ---
ggplot(corr_values, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 4, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#4575b4", mid = "white", high = "#d73027",
    midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "Coeficiente de correlación (r)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Mapa de calor de correlaciones entre Lamud y Molinopampa",
    subtitle = "Asterisco (*) indica correlación significativa (p < 0.05)",
    x = "Lamud",
    y = "Molinopampa"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
```

#### BLUES

```{r}
# Variables de evaluación
vars <- c("pl_height", "days_flower", "n_nod", "w_nod", "disease_sev", "oidio", "pod_weight", "pod_n", "yield" )

df <- inoculation %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_eval), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(.)))))

# Función para ajustar cada modelo
blues_all <- lapply(vars, function(v) {
  # Filtrar datos válidos
  df_sub <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[v]])) %>%
    filter(n_distinct(Site) > 1, n_distinct(treat) > 1)
  
  # Verificar si quedan suficientes datos
  if (nrow(df_sub) < 5) return(NULL)
  
  # Ajustar modelo mixto
  model <- lmer(as.formula(paste(v, "~ Site + treat + (1 | Site:block)")),
                data = df_sub, REML = TRUE)
  
  # Medias ajustadas (BLUEs)
  emm <- emmeans(model, ~ treat | Site)
  df_emm <- as.data.frame(emm)
  df_emm$variable <- v
  df_emm
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

# Agrupar y calcular resumen (incluye desviación estándar)
blues_summary <- blues_all %>%
  group_by(Site, variable) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_emmean = mean(emmean, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(emmean, na.rm = TRUE),
    SE = mean(SE, na.rm = TRUE),
    df = mean(df, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower.CL = mean(lower.CL, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper.CL = mean(upper.CL, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(emmean, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(emmean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(blues_summary)
```


# References

Broman, K. W., & Woo, K. H. (2017). Data organization in spreadsheets. The American Statistician, 72(1), 2–10. https://doi.org/10.1080/00031305.2017.1375989

Zuur, A. F., Ieno, E. N., & Elphick, C. S. (2009). A protocol for data exploration to avoid common statistical problems. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 1(1), 3–14. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.2041-210x.2009.00001.x

Francoishusson. (2017, July 13). PCA course using FactoMineR | R-bloggers. R-bloggers. https://www.r-bloggers.com/2017/07/pca-course-using-factominer/

Kozak, M., & Piepho, H. (2017). What’s normal anyway? Residual plots are more telling than significance tests when checking ANOVA assumptions. Journal of Agronomy and Crop Science, 204(1), 86–98. https://doi.org/10.1111/jac.12220

Tanaka, E., & Hui, F. K. C. (2019). Symbolic formulae for linear mixed models. In Communications in computer and information science (pp. 3–21). https://doi.org/10.1007/978-981-15-1960-4_1

Schielzeth, H., Dingemanse, N. J., Nakagawa, S., Westneat, D. F., Allegue, H., Teplitsky, C., Réale, D., Dochtermann, N. A., Garamszegi, L. Z., & Araya‐Ajoy, Y. G. (2020). Robustness of linear mixed‐effects models to violations of distributional assumptions. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 11(9), 1141–1152. https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210x.13434
